title: Bar Jaraniz
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Structure |image= |name=Bar Jaraniz |constructed=[[Standard year/Legends|Years]] prior to [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]]''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' |destroyed= |type= |location=[[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] |interest= |rebuilt= |builder= |height=Single story |width= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|I seek allies in this filthy place.|[[Nom Anor]]|}} '''Bar Jaraniz'''—also known simply as the '''Jara''''—was a seedy [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcaf]] located on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], the so-called "[[Smuggler/Legends|Smuggler's]] [[Moon/Legends|Moon]]" of [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]. Situated in one of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] rougher neighborhoods, the Jara' vowed never to close, codifying that sentiment in a sign hung over its permanently welded-open doors. The tapcaf served a variety of beverages, including [[Water/Legends|water]] and [[Ale/Legends|ale]], in addition to fanciful [[Cocktail/Legends|cocktails]] that were constructed right before a customer's [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Attracting an eclectic clientele, often made up of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]], and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], Bar Jaraniz weathered its fair share of gang violence and other [[Lightfight|shoot-outs]] over the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], but managed to remain true to its advertised promise of remaining open for business. In the year [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] agent [[Nom Anor]] traveled to Bar Jaraniz in [[Ooglith masquer|disguise]] and under the alias "Udelen," seeking to hire a mercenary's services. There, he met with a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] by the name of [[Goran Beviin]]. After some initial trepidation, Beviin agreed to work for Anor, unaware of his true nature or [[Yuuzhan Vong War|agenda]]; the two exchanged [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|payment]] and assignment details, before the Yuuzhan Vong agent subsequently departed the Jara'. Though his business was concluded, Beviin remained at the tapcaf for some [[Time/Legends|time]] after, in order to join a Mandalorian [[Parent/Legends|mother]] and daughter—[[Briika Jeban|Briika]] and [[Dinua Jeban]]—who'd come to Bar Jaraniz that evening in order to celebrate the young Dinua's recent [[Verd'goten|coming of age]]. ==Description== {{Quote|So this is a tapcaf. This is where I pretend to be an infidel and speak reasonably to abominations.|Nom Anor|}} Bar Jaraniz was located on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta's]] colloquially-named "[[Smuggler/Legends|Smuggler's]] [[Moon/Legends|Moon]]," situated in the region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] known as [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' An unsavory [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcaf]] often referred to simply as the Jara', the establishment's outer facade showed signs of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-charring by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], especially around its doorframe. A sign over the Jara's entrance indicated that the tapcaf never closed, a promise that was seemingly ensured by the fact that the bar's doors had been welded permanently open, though only the owner could truly say what the reason behind such a decision was. Customers of Bar Jaraniz could park their vehicles in a nearby [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] lot, which featured enough space to accommodate vessels as large as a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]]-ready [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]. The interior of Bar Jaraniz featured a variety of seating options for patrons to choose from, from tables with chairs, to high-backed, dimly-lit booths lining at least two of the tapcaf's walls. Customers could also take a seat at the Jara's long serving counter. The Jara' served several beverages, including simple [[Glass/Legends|glasses]] of [[Water/Legends|water]], [[Ale/Legends|ale]] by the mug or bottle, and elaborate [[Cocktail/Legends|cocktails]] made fresh at the counter by the tapcaf's [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]]. [[Jaraniz cocktail|One cocktail]] that Bar Jaraniz offered featured [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]] segments, skewers, and a serving of sky-[[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Vosh/Legends|vosh]] with lumps of [[geref]]. Bartenders at the Jara' worked in shifts, some lasting from late evening through to dawn. Bar Jaraniz supplied guests with [[Napkin/Legends|napkins]], and the tapcaf possessed a [[Refresher/Legends|refresher]] located in the rear of the establishment. By 24 ABY, the Jara' had established a special Buy-One-Get-One-Free night in the fifth [[Standard month/Legends|month]] of the year. The Jara' wasn't known for being crowded on a typical evening, though the tapcaf could count numerous local [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]] among its regular clientele. In addition, Bar Jaraniz was frequented by [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] who would come in search of [[Currency/Legends|paying]] work. Due to the often rough nature of the tapcaf's crowd, patrons customarily made all efforts not to stare at their peers or get involved in another's business for fear of being [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibrobladed]]. The Jara's management was apparently proud of the bar's strict, unwritten etiquette on that matter, despite it ultimately failing to stop a number of minor skirmishes between patrons over the years. ==History== {{Quote|Look, I don't want any trouble.''"
"''Not planning any.|The [[Unidentified Bar Jaraniz bartender|Jaraniz's bartender]] and [[Goran Beviin]]|}} Established on Nar Shaddaa many years for the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]] of the known [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], Bar Jaraniz developed into a seedy tapcaf that often attracted a rough clientele of criminals and mercenaries. In addition to acquiring the simplified Jara' nickname, the bar endured a number of gang wars, [[Lightfight|shoot-outs]], and other armed disagreements between business partners over the course of its operation. In the fifth month of the year 24 ABY, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Parent/Legends|mother]] [[Briika Jeban]] brought her daughter [[Dinua Jeban|Dinua]] to Bar Jaraniz on an unusually crowded Buy-One-Get-One-Free night. Dinua had recently passed her [[Verd'goten|''verd'goten'']], the traditional trial heralding a Mandalorian's coming-of-age, and the two [[Gender/Legends|women]] engaged in a rowdy celebration at the Jara' that evening. While the mother and daughter [[Alcohol/Legends|drank]] and enjoyed each other's company, the Jara's [[Unidentified Bar Jaraniz bartender|bartender]] soon welcomed another Mandalorian by the name of [[Goran Beviin]] to the tapcaf. Arriving in search of a prospective client known as "Udelen," Beviin was directed to a nearby booth by the barkeep, where he unknowingly sat down with a [[Ooglith masquer|disguised]] [[Nom Anor]], an advanced operative of the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|exta-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. Keeping his true identity and intent hidden, Anor attempted to hire Beviin to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] [[Tholote B'Leph]], a supposedly corrupt [[Politics/Legends|politician]] on [[Ter Abbes]]. Tensions rose between the two when Beviin realized the [[Ter Abbes civil war|strife]] that would ensue should B'Leph [[Death/Legends|die]] in accordance with Anor's wishes, and a suspicious Beviin even drew his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster]] on the other man when Anor reached below their table in a manner consistent with someone going for a weapon. Though Briika Jeban was prepared to come to Beviin's aid, the situation was ultimately defused as a misunderstanding, and Beviin temporarily left the Jara' to consider Anor's proposal. Returning to his [[Gladiator-class assault fighter|''Gladiator''-class assault fighter]], which he'd left at a nearby lot, Beviin [[Comm unit/Legends|contacted]] his friend and the reigning [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. After a brief discussion in which Fett urged Beviin to accept the job, as both he and his [[Medrit Vasur|husband]] were in need of the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] after the difficult year their [[Beviin-Vasur farm|farm]] had, Beviin returned to the Jara' and agreed to Anor's terms. Satisfied, the Yuuzhan Vong operative departed Bar Jaraniz without ever touching the glass of water he'd been served upon arrival. Beviin remained behind, however, ordering drinks for the Mandalorian mother and daughter still in the midst of celebrating. At their table, he congratulated Dinua on passing her ''verd'goten'' and offered up a [[Toast (ritual)/Legends|toast]], as was Mandalorian custom, before discussing the recent loss of Dinua's father. Sympathetic, Beviin volunteered to pass along any excess work that came his way to his two new friends. ==Behind the scenes== Bar Jaraniz was first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe in the [[New Jedi Order era]] [[EBook|e-novella]] entitled ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'', written by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]] and published in [[August]] [[2006]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Bar Jaraniz }} [[Category:Nar Shaddaa locations]] [[Category:Tapcafes]]
